summary:
The patient presents with a new type of chest pain occurring unpredictably even while resting; these features are indicative of unstable angina requiring urgent medical evaluation due to its association with higher risk compared to typical forms like stable angina which follows an expected trigger such as physical activity.
The hallmark symptom here—chest discomfort appearing suddenly without clear cause—is central to diagnosing unstable angina necessitating prompt treatment given increased likelihood of serious complications including myocardial infarction relative to more chronic presentations where patterns can be better managed through lifestyle modifications alongside medications if needed but not typically emergent unless severely symptomatic already indicating advanced disease states warranting closer monitoring by healthcare providers immediately upon presentation regardless whether hospital admission becomes necessary based solely off initial assessment findings alone. To summarize concisely:

- Sudden onset unlinked to effort
- Worsening/progressive nature 
- Urgency for rapid intervention indicated Clinical correlation established between described scenario & textbook definition of unstable angina highlighting need for expedited care despite no mention yet made about specific treatments/interventions required beyond recognizing condition exists acutely now vs previously asymptomatic state potentially masking underlying pathology until precipitated abruptly leading directly into current episode prompting visit. Agent will proceed next step focusing specifically addressing appropriate management strategies going forward after confirming diagnosis first achieved above per outlined plan initially set forth during consultation phase prior initiating any therapeutic actions aimed ultimately preventing progression toward worse outcomes associated most notably MI among others possible adverse events depending severity context individual case specifics involved overall health status etc...
